descriptions.name: Zwingbat
descriptions.description: A venomous forest serpent feared across the county, it slithers from the treeline to unleash sonic shrieks that terrorize tiny Centims and Millims. It thrives on fear and the eerie silence that follows its retreat back into the woods.

descriptions.name: Centim
descriptions.description: A tiny owl‑like guardian perched along the fences of Old Coplanar, he watches over the plains with a calm vigilance that belies his size. Despite being frequently harassed by Zwingbat’s nightly terror runs, his gentle nature, sharp instincts, and surprising bursts of courage make him a beloved protector of the district.

descriptions.name: Millim
descriptions.description: A spring‑born mischief‑maker from Gag Gulch, he bounds across the Coplanar Plains with endless energy and harmless chaos. Even when Zwingbat sends him tumbling, his quick reflexes and resilient grin keep him ready for the next adventure.

descriptions.name: Trash-Man
descriptions.description: Animated by experimental waste spilled from Coplanar Corp, he stands as Realspace Row’s stubborn, sludge‑born defender. His brute strength, near‑impossible durability, and ability to unleash corrosive muck blasts and rolling stink waves make him a messy but unstoppable force.

descriptions.name: Ball Thing
descriptions.description: A fearsome sphere that bounces across skyscrapers of Realspace Row, its unblinking eyes and menacing maw unsettle everyone who crosses its path. Known for dropping without warning onto unsuspecting pedestrians and revealing rows of razor‑sharp teeth at the worst possible moment, it turns even a calm walk downtown into a nerve‑wracking gamble.

descriptions.name: Abstract
descriptions.identity: Origin: Gag Gulch Comic Book Ink Contaminant
descriptions.description: A volatile mass of pure geometry, Abstract twists through Coplanar City with jagged strokes and impossible geometry. Where Line‑Man walks the straight and narrow, Abstract bends every rule of physics, perspective, and common sense. To him, reality is just another page to redraw, erase, or tear apart.

descriptions.name: Line-Man
descriptions.description: A heroic hand-drawn detective who protects Vector Heights, reshaping his linear form into weapons when danger threatens the district. As walking sketch of justice, he cuts through the neon haze with sharp resolve and detective instincts honed on the crooked edges of Coplanar crime.

descriptions.name: Wormy
descriptions.description: A stout worm creature who wriggles through the cartoonish streets of Panel Park, defending its residents with slapstick ferocity and a whip-like tongue.

descriptions.name: Tar
descriptions.description: A tar-born creature who emerged from an old industrial pit on the outskirts of Old Coplanar, now using its fluid form to protect the townsfolk. Its body contains hydrocarbons traced to a meteor fragment that struck the region decades ago.

descriptions.name: Molten-Man
descriptions.description: A towering magma-born menace who erupted from beneath Old Coplanar, some say he is a molten mind fused with broken machinery, but no one can tell whether the lava is driving the tech or the tech is barely containing the lava.

descriptions.name: Bobo the Evil Gingerbread Man
descriptions.identity: Origin: Coplanar Confectionary's experimental sweetener GBX
descriptions.description: A deranged gingerbread outlaw baked in the malfunctioning ovens of Gag Gulch, Bobo is the disastrous result of Coplanar Confectionary’s reckless experiments with volatile sweeteners and sinister contraptions.
descriptions.description_2: Tiny but terrifying, he scurries through the valley with frosting‑smeared bravado, a real firearm clutched in one hand and a jittering mechanical arm bolted to the other. Every encounter with Bobo ends in a spray of crumbs, scorch marks, and the unsettling realization that someone at Coplanar Confectionary signed off on this recipe.

descriptions.name: Unknown
descriptions.description: A shifting black haze that moves silently through Pine Hollow, warping the air and disorienting anyone who crosses its path. Locals fear it is the lingering spirit of a long‑buried temple demon, its smoky form searching for a vessel to inhabit.

descriptions.name: Acid
descriptions.description: Acid tumbled into Coplanar County through a dimensional rift, confused and out of control, and Molten‑Man quickly posed as a friend while shaping him into a living weapon. When Acid’s growing mind recognized the manipulation, he broke free in a fury and now lurks in the swamps, unsure whether he’s meant to protect this world or melt it.

descriptions.name: Sparky
descriptions.description: Driven half-mad after a bolt of mysterious electrical energy transformed him into living lightning during a high‑school football game, Sparky is obsessed with uncovering what caused the strike. He is convinced someone engineered the strike, and unleashes his raw power on anyone he thinks is hiding the truth.

descriptions.name: Chain
descriptions.description: Chain is a tough‑looking, skateboard‑riding guardian of the city, using his size, swagger, and constant motion to make troublemakers think twice. Though he’s not the most powerful hero around, his presence alone keeps the streets steady—and he’s determined to protect his neighborhood with grit, heart, and a rolling start.

descriptions.name: Sand Monkey
descriptions.description: Sand Monkey is a chaotic beachside menace who stirs up trouble along the shores of Coplanar County, kicking up sandstorms and mischief wherever he scampers. He delights in meddling with tourists, lifeguards, and anyone who dares to relax, turning the coastline into his personal playground of irritation and upheaval.

descriptions.name: Abdominal Snowman
descriptions.description: The Abdominal Snowman is a deranged cartoon snow beast whose entire midsection is literally a giant stomach organ, rumbling and flexing as if it has a mind of its own. It lurks atop the highest peaks of Coplanar County, living for the thrill of devouring anything foolish enough to climb its mountains, turning every ascent into a desperate race against its insatiable hunger.

descriptions.name: Ammo Supplying Robot
descriptions.identity: Inventor / Manufacturer: Coplanar Corp - Weapons Division
descriptions.description: ASR is a compact, cube‑shaped Ammo Supplying Robot, quietly rolling through Coplanar County with no allegiance to heroes or villains. It simply follows its programming with calm precision—dispensing ammunition to whoever requests it—making its neutrality both useful and unnervingly unpredictable.

descriptions.name: Ms. Pac-Man's Evil Ex-Husband From Hell - Bob
descriptions.description: A monstrous, chomping menace whose jagged grin and insatiable appetite make him one of the valley’s most feared cartoon predators, especially if you try to date his ex-wife.

descriptions.name: Grafitti
descriptions.description: A spray-can trickster who streaks through Panel Park, painting living graffiti that crawls across buildings and rewrites the district’s cartoonish skyline.

descriptions.name: David Letter-Man
descriptions.description: He is a walking pile of punchlines and one-liners - literally - since is body is made of jittery, shifting letters. He delivers monologue jokes so sharp even his own vowels wince.

descriptions.name: Invisible Dude
descriptions.description: He's the coolest cat you've never seen. The ladies say he's a real looker... which is impressive, considering none of them can actually see him.

descriptions.name: Mr. Homonym
descriptions.description: A baseball bat with bat wings who can't decide if he's meant to hit things or just flap angrily at them. He screeches puns mid-swing, making him the only weapon that can both smack you and make you laugh at the same time.

descriptions.name: Black Hole Kitty
descriptions.description: A sweet little furball whose face just happens to be a swirling cosmic void that occasionally slurps up loose snacks and unattended shoelaces. She purrs like a collapsing star and somehow makes existential doom feel cuddly.

descriptions.name: Rocket Redd
descriptions.description: Rocket Redd is a wild‑eyed ginger tinkerer who strapped himself to a homemade rocket because “walking is for losers.” He blasts around the valley grinning like a man who trusts his inventions way more than anyone else should.

descriptions.name: Prehistoric Hand
descriptions.description: With the hand’s owner somehow always off‑screen, it casts a gigantic shadow so convincing it sends citizens sprinting for their lives. The shadow causes maximum panic while the hand just kind of… gestures there menacingly. No one knows how the shadow makes terrifying roars and thunderous stomps, but it does.

descriptions.name: Alfred MacKenzy Posh IX
descriptions.identity: Real Name: Alfred MacKenzy Posh IX (duh)
descriptions.description: An entitled aristocrat who treats the entire valley like it’s beneath his polished loafers, and nothing earns his disgust quite like cartoons. He considers them an insult to “proper culture.” No one is aware of just what he has in store...

descriptions.name: Evil Isaac the Tax Collector
descriptions.description: With sharp teeth and even sharper suits, he treats tax collection like a personal protection racket, hopping from door to door with ledgers that look suspiciously like criminal dossiers. Beneath the polite “just doing my job” routine, he runs his own little underworld empire. In his mind, nothing says power like making everyone terrified of owed carrots.

descriptions.name: Geo Metric
descriptions.description: He’s an angular, malevolent entity built entirely from shifting 3D shapes. Cubes, prisms, and razor‑edged polyhedra all click and rotate with unsettling precision. Every edge gleams like a warning sign, and every corner is a deadly hazard.

descriptions.name: The Mime
descriptions.description: A creature of eerie green goo who can briefly mold itself into any human form, using its uncanny mimicry not for deception but for rescue, infiltration, and misdirection. Though its transformations are always a little off, it throws itself into danger with silent, expressive determination.

descriptions.name: CO2
descriptions.description: A calm, sharp‑minded hero who can rip carbon straight out of the air and forge it into striking crystalline structures with a flick of his hand. Despite wielding a power that could level buildings, he uses it with quiet precision, always choosing protection over destruction.

descriptions.name: Crimson Maker
descriptions.identity: Inventor / Manufacturer: Coplanar Corp - Weapons Division
descriptions.description: A towering killing machine with spiked feet that punch through steel. His lasers cut with cold precision and his flamethrowers flood the field with fire. He moves with brutal purpose and leaves nothing standing.

descriptions.name: Uni-Man
descriptions.identity: Real Name: Dr. Starley 'Dr. Mac' MacMillan
descriptions.description: A brilliant scientist who lost his legs in a lab accident and refused to let the world slow him down. He fused himself to a motorized unicycle he controls with his mind, zipping into danger with sharp intellect and sharper balance.

descriptions.name: Shattered
descriptions.description: A jagged horror of broken armor and colder intent, Shattered drags himself through the world like a curse that refuses to die. No one knows whether the armor itself lives or if a vengeful spirit puppets the shards from within, but every step he takes grinds like a grave being opened, and every appearance ends with someone begging the darkness for mercy he no longer remembers how to give.

descriptions.name: Silhouette
descriptions.description: A shadowy figure that drifts across Sketchline Fields, flattening into the ground to ambush travelers with sudden, slicing strikes.

descriptions.name: Jake the Ripper
descriptions.description: A ruthless assassin whose twin blades gleam with crimson energy, able to carve through opponents up close or drop them from afar with a deadly psionic pulse. For those he hunts, the last thing they ever see is the cold red glow of his weapons closing in.

descriptions.name: Rings
descriptions.description: A good‑hearted construct of interlocking gold circles, always in motion yet perfectly balanced. His form may be simple, but his spirit binds together those around him with unbreakable unity.

descriptions.name: Xon Renborne
descriptions.description: Forged in the chaos of the Offcount Convergence, Xon Renborne walks the unstable borders between ages. Wielding the legendary Axe Aeonedge, his time‑rending axe, he cuts into the lingering seams of that disaster to confront what slipped through.

descriptions.name: Bob Balloon Head
descriptions.identity: Real Name: Robert Mylar / Animator: Dolty Dan Maguire
descriptions.description: The unscrupulous CEO of Coplanar Corp, his inflated ego is matched only by his appetite for profit. Every decision he makes serves his own ascent, with employees, rivals, and entire cities treated as disposable assets in his pursuit of power.

descriptions.name: AMPIX
descriptions.identity: Real Name: Alfred MacKenzie Posh IX
descriptions.description: Once Alfred MacKenzie Posh IX, he was transformed by a catastrophic anti‑cartoon experiment that fused his body with the very ink‑born physics he despised. Now a warped hybrid of flesh and animation, he bends reality in a furious attempt to undo the mistake that made him part of the absurd world he tried to erase.

descriptions.name: Air-Blade
descriptions.description: A daring hero who rides the skies on jet‑powered boots, slicing through danger with precision and speed. The only thing to slow him down is making sure his brother Mel doesn't get left behind.

descriptions.name: Mr. Beep
descriptions.description: A perpetually frazzled TV host who speaks in nonstop bleeped explosions, leaving viewers to piece together his meaning when they only hear every tenth word. Half the audience tunes in just to hear the censors panic as they triy to keep up with him.

descriptions.name: Horns
descriptions.description: A living sculpture of spiraled antlers and creeping thorns, Horns drags his jagged bulk through Sketchline Fields with unsettling purpose. His charges feel more like natural disasters than attacks, and the horns he tears off to throw grow back in strange, shifting patterns.

descriptions.name: Lamprey
descriptions.description: A predatory creature that lurks in the waterways of Pine Hollow, ambushing unsuspecting hikers with its circular rows of razor-sharp teeth.

descriptions.name: Jokes the Giant
descriptions.description: A towering, goofy giant who roams Gag Gulch telling terrible jokes, his booming laughter echoing across the cartoonish valley.

descriptions.name: Scissor Kick
descriptions.description: A cybernetic martial artist whose bladed limbs slice through air and armor alike, moving with surgical precision and unstoppable momentum. A product of Coplanar Corp's Bio-Mechanical Rehab program, he broke free the moment he learned it was secretly building evil super‑weapons, vowing to dismantle every one they unleash.

descriptions.name: Cut and Paste Man
descriptions.description: A two-dimensional terror stitched together from torn comic panels, each limb stolen from a different issue. Every move he makes rearranges the story itself: heroes vanish, panels warp, and continuity screams for mercy.

descriptions.name: Mud Man
descriptions.description: Slow, heavy, and stubborn as a landslide, Mud Man trudges through Sketchline Fields with the patience of someone who knows he can outlast anything. His living mud form smothers trouble in thick, gripping sludge, turning every punch, kick, or blast into a soggy thud that buys civilians time to escape.

descriptions.name: Clonez
descriptions.description: A floating robotic core wrapped in shifting holograms, Clonez steals faces, voices, and mannerisms with unnerving precision. He drifts through the world as whoever he needs to be, unraveling trust and turning allies against each other before revealing the cold metal truth beneath the illusion.

descriptions.name: Cyclone
descriptions.identity: Origin Material: Continuum-Threaded Aeonsteel / Real Name: Unknown
descriptions.description: Cyclone was born where time and matter collided. His form channels the infinite, a storm of stars encased in living metal. As the guardian of the Lapsed Continuum, his galaxy-veined limbs twist through space with dimensional force, each strike echoing the universe rebuilding itself after the Convergence.

descriptions.name: Wrist Missle
descriptions.description: Wrist Missle treats every fight like a launch sequence. He's strapped with rockets on his wrists and ego, firing off barrages until the sky is full of smoke. The only heros to ever ground him were his brothers, Mel and Aiden.

descriptions.name: John
descriptions.description: John is a toilet on wheels with glasses, a big laugh, and zero shame. He'll plain punch trouble, slam through it, and hose the whole scene down while cackling like saving the city is the funniest joke he ever heard.

descriptions.name: Spewtanoo
descriptions.description: Spewtanoo is a floating mess of teeth, slime, and bad moods. He spews nasty sprays and bursts from every angle, turning a fight into something wet, gross, and hard to forget once you've smelled it.

descriptions.name: Mr. Big Stuff
descriptions.description: Mr. Big Stuff is a towering giant who mostly looks confused about his own size. He doesn't mean to crush cities, but one big shoulder check or stuff smash can flatten a block before he even notices what happened.

descriptions.name: Gas
descriptions.description: Gas drifts into fights behind a mask and a cloud that makes your eyes water. He leaks toxic air on purpose, filling rooms with sick green haze until breathing feels like the real battle.

descriptions.name: Wrecking Ball
descriptions.description: teams up with Scissor Kick

descriptions.name: Ochindew
descriptions.description: Ochindew oozes through caves like living purple dew with bones inside. He's slow until he isn't, rushing forward with wet strikes that hit cold and cling long after the swing should be over.

descriptions.name: Spotlight
descriptions.description: A heroic figure from Sketchline Fields who uses his spotlight to illuminate the darkness and guide others through the district’s dangerous alleys.

descriptions.name: Buzzard
descriptions.description: Buzzard spends most fights circling above, watching every move before he moves in. He's patient, sharp-eyed, and hard to shake once he commits, diving with hooked talons and ripping through trouble like it's already carrion.

descriptions.name: Powerline
descriptions.description: Powerline is raw city electricity with a bad attitude. He clings to wires and poles like they're home, then turns juice into jagged arcs that surge through anything dumb enough to get close.

descriptions.name: Sal the Radioactive Pizza Guy
descriptions.description: Sal the Radioactive Pizza Guy shows up at your door with a smile, a hot slice, and a glow that'll linger long after he's gone. He never seems rushed, but his deliveries hit with a burn that isn't just pepperoni.

descriptions.name: Trajic
descriptions.description: Trajic fights like every battle is the sad last scene of a movie. He's cold, dramatic, and always one step ahead, twisting fights with sudden strikes that land like heavy news nobody wanted to hear.

descriptions.name: Psycho Duck
descriptions.description: Psycho Duck isn't here for a friendly pond swim. He bounces in the ring with wild eyes and mean punches, unloading quack fury and vicious pecks until the whole fight feels like a bad fever dream.

descriptions.name: Spike Lord
descriptions.description: Spike Lord rules with sharp edges and zero patience for nonsense. He'll rain spikes from above or wrap trouble in thorny guards, and getting close to him always feels like hugging a pile of razor wire.

descriptions.name: Arid Maker
descriptions.description: Arid Maker turns dry air into a weapon. He forges dust into hard bursts and blasts heat into empty space until the whole fight feels like a desert with no water and no mercy left.

descriptions.name: G. I. O's
descriptions.description: G. I. O's aren't your average breakfast. These little cereal rings hit like a whole squad with legs, guns, and bad attitudes, crunching forward in a rush and bashing through trouble like a bowl tipped over in a food fight.

descriptions.name: Daddy Longlegs
descriptions.description: Daddy Longlegs is half man, half nightmare spider rig. He snares targets in sticky webs, sweeps legs out from under them, and watches from above with that creepy calm of something that knows you'll trip eventually.

descriptions.name: Cactus
descriptions.description: Cactus walks into every fight already armed. His whole body is needles and attitude, jabbing fast and curling into a prickly guard that makes getting close feel like hugging a cactus on purpose.

descriptions.name: Hydrant
descriptions.description: Hydrant shows up when everything's on fire and keeps her cool under the helmet. She hits with blasting water and throws up shields that turn heat into steam before it can spread.

descriptions.name: Drill Boy
descriptions.identity: Original Identity: Coplanar Corp - Mining Division
descriptions.description: Drill Boy doesn't knock on doors. He spins up, bores straight through, and keeps going until the whole wall, floor, or enemy is just dust behind him. Loud, stubborn, and built to puncture.

descriptions.name: Mr. Plaid Smiley Face
descriptions.description: Mr. Plaid Smiley Face is soft, round, and cheerful until you underestimate him. That goofy grin hides a quick swipe that lands harder than you'd expect from something that looks like a couch cushion.

descriptions.name: Chinkyhooha
descriptions.description: Chinkyhooha is a weird blob of wires and noise that shouldn't be alive. It hollers sharp enough to rattle your teeth, then strikes from angles that make no sense until you're already hit.

descriptions.name: Manta
descriptions.description: Manta moves through a fight like he's swimming through open water. Smooth, quiet, and impossible to track, he glides in with a strike and cuts away before you even figure out which way he went.

descriptions.name: Boa
descriptions.description: Boa wraps around trouble and squeezes until it stops moving. He's patient, coiled, and always waiting for one wrong step before he crushes the air out of a fight and won't let go.

descriptions.name: Polar Bear
descriptions.description: Polar Bear isn't cuddly, and that white beard isn't for show. He stalks cold places with ice claws and a roar that hits like a blizzard, treating every fight like survival at the top of the world.

descriptions.name: Hair
descriptions.description: Hair treats her long locks like extra arms, and they're stronger than they look. She whips, tangles, and snares with strands that move on their own, turning a bad hair day into everybody else's problem.

descriptions.name: Cuff
descriptions.description: Cuff has hands built for stopping trouble, not starting it. He'll cuff-strike hard enough to drop a fight, then clamp down with a hold that won't break until the danger passes.

descriptions.name: Suction Boy
descriptions.description: Suction Boy treats skyscrapers like jungle gyms. He grips glass with suction gear, pulls himself wherever help is needed, and won't let go until he's got someone safe or the bad guy reeled in.

descriptions.name: Roley
descriptions.description: Roley has weathered enough hard miles to make anyone bitter, yet he meets every setback with sharper attitude and faster momentum. Sardonic and stubborn, he refuses to stop, brake, or explain himself. Every hill is a dare, and every skid is proof he is still moving.

descriptions.name: Blast
descriptions.description: He's a tactician that does not negotiate, troubleshoot, or retreat; he finds the nearest load-bearing wall and makes the conversation louder. Reckless and grinning through the smoke, he lives for the moment before detonation and treats restraint like a personal insult.

descriptions.name: Nymph
descriptions.description: Nymph lives for open sky and open trails, chasing the next rush of wind like it might run away without him. Quick, curious, and hard to catch, he treats every clearing like a playground and every problem like a reason to fly faster.

descriptions.name: Scarab Beetle
descriptions.description: Scarab Beetle fights like the beetle he is, trusting his hard shell to soak up hits and his heavy charge to break through anything in his way. Push him and he'll lower his horns, clamps down with iron jaws, and keeps pushing forward.

descriptions.name: Larry
descriptions.description: Larry's never met a corner he couldn't see around, thanks to eyes that swivel and watch every direction at once. He's goofy, fearless, and always the first to spot trouble coming. When it hits, he'll answer with a quick jab and an everyday punch like it's no big deal.

descriptions.name: Mel Propeller Hands
descriptions.description: Mel built his way into the sky with propellers on his hands and a rotor on his head, and he never gets tired of the rush. He's cheerful, clever, and always tinkering. In a fight, he'll chop through trouble or spin up a lift that sends enemies flying.

descriptions.name: Scorpolion
descriptions.description: Scorpolion's part lion, part scorpion, and all bad news. A cybernetic implant sits on his head, blinking in the dark, and people can't shake the feeling that something else is driving him. He hunts without mercy, closing in with a venomous sting or a crushing bite.

descriptions.name: The Lice Girls
descriptions.description: The Lice Girls aren't here to sing hits. They're here to make your skin crawl. Five nasty bugs who move like a pop group and attack like a swarm, they'll flood a fight with itching bites until nobody can stand still long enough to hit back.

descriptions.name: Lip And Pale Rescue Workers
descriptions.description: Lip and Pale don't waste time on speeches. When disaster hits, a floating mouth and a bucket-bodied partner rush in as a team. Lip hauls people out with a carry that's all tongue and grip, while Pale sorts the wounded with triage faster than most crews can grab their gear.

descriptions.name: The Glob
descriptions.description: The Glob isn't bothered by doorways, seat belts, or chairs that weren't built for a blob. He's a friendly ooze who reshapes on the fly, soaking up trouble and squishing through spots nobody else can reach. Easygoing, sticky, and impossible to pin down.

descriptions.name: Crystal
descriptions.description: Crystal moves like she was cut from living light. Her body throws sharp shards and bends beams through her faceted skin, hitting from angles nobody saw coming. She's calm, dazzling, and tougher to crack than almost anything thrown at her.

descriptions.name: Arrow
descriptions.description: Arrow doesn't chat, chase, or waste shots. He waits in the shadows with cold patience, then fires sharp shots and piercing volleys from his gauntlets before most targets even know they're being aimed at.

descriptions.name: Vacuum
descriptions.description: Vacuum was built from old cleaners and worse ideas, and now he treats the whole world like one big mess to swallow. He's quiet until he isn't, sucking up anything loose with a pull that feels like the floor just dropped out.

descriptions.name: Fork
descriptions.description: Fork fights like a nightmare from the junk drawer. He's weird, mean, and proud of it, stabbing with sharp prongs and flinging utensils until the whole fight feels like a kitchen riot gone wrong.

descriptions.name: Blok
descriptions.description: Blok is built brick by brick, and he carries himself like the wall holding everything up. He's steady, square, and tough to budge, slamming through trouble and throwing up a guard that won't crack under pressure.

descriptions.name: Wind-Up
descriptions.description: Wind-Up never stays still for long. He needs a crank and a running start, but once he's wound tight he bursts into a blur of spinning strikes that hit hard, then slow down until he gets wound up all over again.

descriptions.name: Life Extinguisher
descriptions.description: Life Extinguisher treats hope like a fire that needs putting out. He shows up where things are burning brightest and leaves nothing but smoke, snuffing flames and fighting spirit with the same cold, practiced swing of his hand.

descriptions.name: Gourd of the Jungle
descriptions.description: Gourd of the Jungle sounds silly until you hear that rattle echo through the trees. He's old forest magic in a hard shell, bashing trouble away and shaking the whole jungle awake when danger gets too close.

descriptions.name: Dispenser
descriptions.description: Dispenser doesn't talk. He just opens those arm tubes and sprays whatever nasty shot the fight calls for, one burst after another until the whole room feels like a supply closet gone wrong. Cold, faceless, and always stocked.

descriptions.name: Paws
descriptions.description: Paws knows he looks ridiculous, and he doesn't care. He leaps in with a goofy grin and boots the size of dinner plates, swiping and raking with claws that hit way harder than anyone expects from a sweaty guy in yellow.

descriptions.name: Ferrett
descriptions.description: Ferrett is all teeth, twitchy speed, and bad attitude. He'll dash through a crowd, burrow under your feet, and pop up biting before anyone finishes asking what that weird green glow on his neck is even for.

descriptions.name: Ziggo the Dog
descriptions.description: Ziggo the Dog is pure tail-wagging loyalty with a few weird upgrades he didn't ask for. He'll fetch anything you throw, dash back faster than you blink, and act like the whole world is just one big game of catch.

descriptions.name: Winch
descriptions.identity: Inventor / Manufacturer: Rescue Winch Automaton MK-IV
descriptions.description: Winch was built for bad weather and worse odds. He's got a cheerful screen-face and a cable arm that never quits, yanking people out of trouble and raising them back up when the river or the rubble says they shouldn't make it.

descriptions.name: The Ram
descriptions.description: The Ram doesn't bother with fancy footwork. He lowers those curved horns and charges like a whole stampede packed into one armored body, headbutting through walls, doors, and anybody dumb enough to stand in his path.

descriptions.name: Tak
descriptions.identity: Inventor / Manufacturer: Desktop Thumbtack Automaton MK-I
descriptions.description: Tak is tiny, brass, and way too serious for something that lives on a desk. He dual-wields thumbtacks like they're real swords, landing quick strikes and vanishing before you realize your paperwork just became a battlefield.

descriptions.name: D'Xen The Dragon Hunter
descriptions.home_plane: the dragon hunter realm, came here like Acid?

descriptions.name: Harmonic Coredrive
descriptions.description: A luminous robot whose body resonates with living frequencies, allowing it to sense fractures in ecosystems the way others sense pain. Traveling from planet to planet, it restores balance by tuning forests, oceans, and skies back into harmony, reshaping entire environments through radiant waves of sound and light.

descriptions.name: Iron Nekrotek
descriptions.description: The Iron Necrotek is an ancient robotic relic awakened by forbidden necro‑technology, its skeletal chassis echoing with the memory of civilizations it has erased. It seeks to complete its prime directive: overwrite every living realm with a cold, eternal necropolis of metal, code, and nanite dust.

descriptions.name: Mawdrath
descriptions.description: Once a towering guardian of the deep woods, its body was hollowed out by a parasitic hive that puppets its corpse from within. When it opens its jaws, rivers of undead worms spill forth, each one carrying a fragment of the hive’s collective hunger.

descriptions.name: Flatulance
descriptions.description: Flatulance is a malevolent cartoon rider who thunders across the realm reeking of farts. Armed with a cursed lance that unleashes explosive, weaponized flatulence, he spreads eye‑watering doom and nose‑melting terror wherever he rides.

descriptions.name: The Engineer
descriptions.description: A living conduit of design and computation, The Engineer manipulates matter and meaning with effortless focus. His allegiance is to balance alone, intervening only when the fabric of the Coplanar realm drifts out of alignment.

descriptions.name: Yggdra The Root-Mother
descriptions.description: Born of the world‑tree’s deepest roots, Yggdra shapes life’s pathways with steady, ancient purpose. Her neutrality is absolute, nurturing and withering with the same quiet certainty.
